scores.correctness_notes: Gets the high-level concepts right (supported bases/arches, multi-base omits top-level base, cross-compilation) but the multi-base build-on/build-for syntax is wrong — the agent shows just `[amd64]` in build-on/build-for, whereas for multi-base you must use the full `ubuntu@22.04:amd64` format. Also misses the shorthand `ubuntu@22.04:amd64:` platform key syntax and `ubuntu@devel` as a valid build-base.
scores.hallucination_notes: The multi-base example with `build-on: [amd64]` under a ubuntu-22.04-amd64 platform is factually wrong for multi-base (needs base-qualified arch reference), but this is an inaccuracy rather than a fully fabricated API. `build-base` key is real. No invented APIs detected.
